transcript.text: Good morning.
Sorry, I was on mute.
Oh, you're okay.
I'm just waiting on...
A few people?
It was Jason and Colin.
Colin's out of office today.
Oh.
Yeah.
Oh, okay.
Hmm.
All right.
Well, we can still run through the notes and then probably the biggest piece is use case guides anyways.
So.
Okay.
I'm just going to jump in then.
whenever, whenever Jason joins, but just a quick overview, as always on integration pages, we're looking at, hey, Jason, Colin's out of office today.
So I'm just going to dive in.
Um, but yeah, so nearing 500 publishing, just a quick snapshot on traffic.
A little bit of dip in traffic, but clicks are still pretty healthy and the keywords that matter are doing really well.
So no reason to be concerned at this time.
We did put together a little bit of a deep dive for Colin based on last week's conversation.
So yeah, async, we can, you know, post that and have that important to also look at.
But mostly looking at positions between branded and non-branded searches.
So for brand and search, Webflow keywords are doing really well.
They pretty much own it for the most part.
And so that's looking good.

There's a bit of opportunity around non-branded keywords and sort of some discernments needed in thinking about like this version two optimization step for the integration pages around like how much we want to actually optimize these and whether it's worth it comparing it to an equation.
It's article of an integration piece and everything like that.
So I just pulled out kind of the top ones based on search volume, but there's other ways and kind of vectoring that we can do to look at what's most important.
But that's just something that we'll include into that optimization strategy.
Do you need us to input on any of these or how should we?

I think we'll deliver that final strategy to y'all for approval, but this is just sort of an early look at diving deep into what it would take to improve the traffic or click-through rate for the non-branded prompts.
think there's still a few branded prompts that we could also improve positioning on, so maybe that would be like a bigger priority.
And then after that, like, looking at this as another optional priority, depending on, like, what those competing domains look like, I think.
But yeah, so mostly just your approval once we have that a bit more hashed out.
So yeah, any thoughts?
Any or anything to add to that, Jason, just for?
Yeah, guess just the context there is like the integration page is doing really well.
We're just unpacking what is happening.
So to summarize, it's like you rank really well for when people are actually searching for the integration.
That's what they're looking for is to like Webflow Stripe, Webflow Shopify integration, as you can see.
You know, where this came from was, I think, a week or two, though, circled back.
It's obnoxious of this.
Actually, can't hear you that well.
Sorry, I don't know.
Oh, really?
Hmm.
Is this better?
That's better.
This is better?
Okay.
I'm trying to use my MacBook microphone.
Yeah, so I think a week or two ago, we showed this like blended position, and we're just kind of unpacking.
So what it's showing is you rank really well when people search for Webflow plus a brand like integration.
It's also showing you guys are now getting picked up for things that like almost don't necessarily have to do with this integration.
Like it's not somebody who uses Webflow who's very specifically just like, hey, show me how to integrate.
Right.
It's picking up for things that are kind of in the halo of bad intent.
So like, you know, people looking to add a social feed to Webflow, people who's just looking to migrate from Squarespace to Webflow.
Like these are now being like these integration guys are now being surfaced for that.
It's also surfaced for like just things like Stripe integration or the branded term for like one of your integration partners.
So basically we see this, it informs, you know, our next, like our next generation of these pages, we probably.
And want to rank even better for some of these, right, because I imagine it's helpful for you all to rank higher when somebody thinks about transferring Squarespace to Webflow.
And I want some thoughts there, and you guys can give feedback.
Okay.
Does that make sense?
Yeah.
Cool.
If we can dive into use case guides, before we talk about these articles I sent over to Raymond, I think it's worth just going into, like, how we approach this in general.

So since I believe we've never really done a walkthrough with you, but we have a proprietary tool that is a workflow integrating a few steps, some agents, some fact-checking steps, et cetera, in order to produce content.
And sort of what we're trying to do with any of our accounts is improve that workflow to make it more specific to our needs.
And it's obviously can be very difficult.
It's with just like a one-shot prompt to generate really technical content.
So we go much deeper into all of the different steps it would take to be able to generate that content and then having that human envelope at the end just for QAing it.
So the first step we're taking is codifying your feedback so far.
And so these screenshots are a little bit large, but they're just localized to the text in the Google Sheet.

And so we're putting it here of like, there's obviously room for you to disagree with how we codified them, but it's mostly for our engineering team to give them a very clear snapshot into the quality bar that we're looking at.
So it's kind of an overwhelming size of these screenshots.
And then after that, we move into more of the content engine, building out those specs.
And so that can get pretty in-depth.
So this is just like an example of a table of contents of how we might build out that engineering spec.
And so thinking about, um,
And like, in this case, I don't think we already have done the keyword research ourselves.
So more so, what does our generation look like?
What's our benchmark?
What's our bar?
What's our current input-output?
What do we want that input-output to be?
And so on our end, it takes quite a bit of work to get all of that detail established.
And so that would bring us all the way back to sort of our benchmark articles.
And if you had a chance to take a look at these in terms of technical content, and if this is sort of what we're working towards.
So that's a question for you, Raymond.
Yeah, I looked at a couple of them.
And from what I saw, a couple of them kind of addressed what I was talking about in terms of showing a bit of code.
Not like Python or JavaScript or whatever, but just like a bit of structure, like right there.
That's something I felt that the integration guides could have benefited from.
Yeah.
Just like the SoundCloud one, for example, like, what does a SoundCloud record look like?
Now, which one is that?
This?
Oh, so the SoundCloud one was one of the ones I reviewed a couple weeks ago.
Oh, sorry.
Yeah, of R-Docs.
Okay.
No, it's, yeah, that one took some of my mind.
Okay, so that one would be like a helpful benchmark then.
Yeah.
Let me see if I can pull that one out real quick.
And then any feedback on these?
In terms of the reference?
Or like, this is fine then.
We're like on the right track.
Yeah, so again, like what I'm saying is that I know we don't want to have a full technical blog with soup to nuts creating an application per se.
Like, it's just a little bit more technical content to just kind of get.
I think we're on the right track, we're hoping to get that to engineering today and then we'll have that first version for calibration and then we'll just iterate until we have our final copy.

Great, that's kind of a short call today actually, so we are waiting on the category refreshes if, I know Colin, maybe Vivian, we're working on that, but just we're able to do that ourselves in the CMS.
I don't know if there's any updates that y are aware of in terms of us being able to move forward on reorganizing the categories.
That you have, whether you have the ability to or whether we feel the approval, other approvals.
Yeah.
I don't have, I can ask Vivian really quickly if she's here, if she's out, I don't have any word on that.
I can try to look through Slack really quickly.
I pinged Colin yesterday and so, but I think he said he would mention it to Vivian I think last week.
So yeah, if you could ping her, if she doesn't have context, that's fine, then we can wait, but.
You
Yeah.
And then Developer Hub, this is still very much in progress, and then Partner Update System is still in progress.

I know we had one come through last week for UploadCare or something, and then I think that's already been fixed pretty quickly, but still hoping to establish a new system for that going into the new year.
And I think that's actually about it for this call.
So next week, we won't have a call or the following.
That's like our quiet week.
So we'll move to kind of async work.
But any other thoughts or questions?
Not at the moment.
Thanks for the updates and excited for the recommendations from the deep dive that you all went through.
Great.
All right.
Well, thanks, everyone.
I don't know if you have anything else, Jason, to add before we close.
Nope.
Just have a good holiday break.
You all do well.
Have fun with the family.
I hope we're to San Diego.
Oh, cool.
That'll be nice.
Eight-hour drive with the kid.
We'll see how that goes.
But I'm sure once we get there, it'll be great.
McDonald's is right there, three miles away.
All right, y'all.
Have a good one.
Thank you.
Take care.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on SEO performance and the new use case guide content strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/E_vw_wgVSgi3fxWcvLUZjFntjA3b9BZb?tab=summary&timestamp=157.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Integration pages rank well for branded terms** (e.g., "Webflow Stripe") but also capture high-intent non-branded searches (e.g., "migrate Squarespace to Webflow"), revealing a new SEO opportunity.](https://fathom.video/share/E_vw_wgVSgi3fxWcvLUZjFntjA3b9BZb?tab=summary&timestamp=353.0)
  - [**A new content engine is being built** to produce more technical "use case guides" with code examples, addressing a key feedback gap from the current integration pages.](https://fathom.video/share/E_vw_wgVSgi3fxWcvLUZjFntjA3b9BZb?tab=summary&timestamp=447.0)
  - [**The content engine's benchmark is the SoundCloud guide,** which successfully shows the required level of technical detail (e.g., data structure).](https://fathom.video/share/E_vw_wgVSgi3fxWcvLUZjFntjA3b9BZb?tab=summary&timestamp=556.0)
  - [**The next sync is canceled for the holidays;** work will continue asynchronously.](https://fathom.video/share/E_vw_wgVSgi3fxWcvLUZjFntjA3b9BZb?tab=summary&timestamp=737.0)

## Topics

### Integration Page SEO Deep Dive

  - [**Performance:** Nearing 500 published pages; a recent traffic dip is not a concern, as clicks remain healthy and key keywords perform well.](https://fathom.video/share/E_vw_wgVSgi3fxWcvLUZjFntjA3b9BZb?tab=summary&timestamp=172.0)
  - [**Branded Search:** Webflow dominates branded search terms (e.g., "Webflow Stripe integration").](https://fathom.video/share/E_vw_wgVSgi3fxWcvLUZjFntjA3b9BZb?tab=summary&timestamp=202.0)
  - [**Non-Branded Opportunity:** Pages are capturing high-intent non-branded searches, revealing a new opportunity.](https://fathom.video/share/E_vw_wgVSgi3fxWcvLUZjFntjA3b9BZb?tab=summary&timestamp=353.0)
      - [**Examples:** "add social feed to Webflow," "migrate Squarespace to Webflow," "Stripe integration."](https://fathom.video/share/E_vw_wgVSgi3fxWcvLUZjFntjA3b9BZb?tab=summary&timestamp=370.0)
      - [**Significance:** This indicates users are finding Webflow for broader, high-intent tasks, not just specific integrations.](https://fathom.video/share/E_vw_wgVSgi3fxWcvLUZjFntjA3b9BZb?tab=summary&timestamp=370.0)
  - [**Next Steps:** GrowthX will deliver a formal "version two" optimization strategy for Webflow approval.](https://fathom.video/share/E_vw_wgVSgi3fxWcvLUZjFntjA3b9BZb?tab=summary&timestamp=256.0)

### New Content Engine for Use Case Guides

  - [**Goal:** Address a key feedback gap by producing more technical content than the current integration pages.](https://fathom.video/share/E_vw_wgVSgi3fxWcvLUZjFntjA3b9BZb?tab=summary&timestamp=574.0)
  - [**Methodology:** A proprietary content engine with a multi-step workflow (agents, fact-checking, human QA) to generate technical content at scale.](https://fathom.video/share/E_vw_wgVSgi3fxWcvLUZjFntjA3b9BZb?tab=summary&timestamp=447.0)
  - [**Process:**](https://fathom.video/share/E_vw_wgVSgi3fxWcvLUZjFntjA3b9BZb?tab=summary&timestamp=447.0)
    1.  [**Codify Feedback:** Convert specific feedback into engineering specs to set a clear quality bar.](https://fathom.video/share/E_vw_wgVSgi3fxWcvLUZjFntjA3b9BZb?tab=summary&timestamp=493.0)
    2.  [**Build Specs:** Create detailed engineering specs (e.g., TOCs, input/output benchmarks) to guide the engine.](https://fathom.video/share/E_vw_wgVSgi3fxWcvLUZjFntjA3b9BZb?tab=summary&timestamp=523.0)
    3.  [**Generate & Iterate:** Use the engine to produce content for calibration and refinement.](https://fathom.video/share/E_vw_wgVSgi3fxWcvLUZjFntjA3b9BZb?tab=summary&timestamp=662.0)
  - [**Benchmark:** The SoundCloud guide is the target for technical depth, as it successfully shows data structure (e.g., "what a SoundCloud record looks like").](https://fathom.video/share/E_vw_wgVSgi3fxWcvLUZjFntjA3b9BZb?tab=summary&timestamp=556.0)

### Project Status & Logistics

  - [**Category Refreshes:** Blocked pending approval from Vivian.](https://fathom.video/share/E_vw_wgVSgi3fxWcvLUZjFntjA3b9BZb?tab=summary&timestamp=678.0)
  - [**Developer Hub:** Still in progress.](https://fathom.video/share/E_vw_wgVSgi3fxWcvLUZjFntjA3b9BZb?tab=summary&timestamp=721.0)
  - [**Partner Update System:** Still in progress; a recent UploadCare issue was fixed quickly.](https://fathom.video/share/E_vw_wgVSgi3fxWcvLUZjFntjA3b9BZb?tab=summary&timestamp=721.0)
  - [**Sync Schedule:** The next two weekly syncs are canceled for the holidays.](https://fathom.video/share/E_vw_wgVSgi3fxWcvLUZjFntjA3b9BZb?tab=summary&timestamp=737.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX:** Deliver the "version two" integration page optimization strategy for Webflow approval.](https://fathom.video/share/E_vw_wgVSgi3fxWcvLUZjFntjA3b9BZb?tab=summary&timestamp=256.0)
  - [**GrowthX:** Deliver the first batch of use case guides for calibration and feedback.](https://fathom.video/share/E_vw_wgVSgi3fxWcvLUZjFntjA3b9BZb?tab=summary&timestamp=662.0)
  - [**Webflow:** Follow up with Vivian to unblock the category refreshes.](https://fathom.video/share/E_vw_wgVSgi3fxWcvLUZjFntjA3b9BZb?tab=summary&timestamp=678.0)

action_items.description: Post integration pages deep dive (incl. non-branded opps) for Colin
Send use case guides spec to engineering; then deliver 1st-version guides for calibration
Ping Vivian re: CMS category refresh; then update Liz on status
